name: Almost Perfect Cheesecake
recipeIngredient: 1 can (1 lb) sour pitted cherries packed in water
recipeInstructions:
1. Crush wafers until finely ground.
2. Melt butter and mix with wafers for the crust. Press into a 10-inch springform pan using fingers, ensuring the crust is as thin as possible, especially at the corners.
3. Filling: Blend cream cheese, sugar, and lemon juice thoroughly. Add eggs and blend well. Pour mixture into the crust and bake at 350°F for 30-45 minutes until set (not solid), slightly golden, and perhaps split on top.
4. Remove from oven and cool on a rack. Allow it to firm up more while cooling (about 45 minutes).
5. Topping: Mix sour cream, sugar, and vanilla. Pour over the cooled cake. Return to the oven at 350°F for 30 minutes. Cool to room temperature and refrigerate until thoroughly chilled (overnight is fine) but at least 5-6 hours.
6. Glaze (optional): Drain cherries, reserving liquid. In a saucepan, combine sugar and cornstarch, adding cherry liquid and stirring until smooth. Bring to a boil over medium heat for 1 minute until thick and clear. Remove from heat, let cool slightly, then add lemon juice, cherries, and food coloring. Cool until warm but pourable. Pour over the chilled cheesecake and refrigerate for an hour or so before serving.
Freezes well.
